# Analyzing Tunnel Log Traffic Using Splunk SIEM

## Introduction

**Tunnel logs generated by _Zeek IDS (formerly Bro)_ provide visibility into tunneling protocols such as GRE, IPv4, and IPv6. Analyzing these logs using Splunk helps security teams monitor hidden traffic flows, detect anomalies, and identify potential threats like covert channels or data exfiltration.**

## Project Overview

**This project demonstrates how to ingest and analyze tunnel log data from Zeek IDS in Splunk SIEM to gain insights into tunneling behavior and detect suspicious activity within a network.**

## Prerequisites

**Before starting, ensure:**

***Splunk is installed and configured***
***Zeek IDS tunnel logs are available or being forwarded to Splunk***

# Uploading Tunnel Logs to Splunk

 >***1. Prepare Log Files - ( I have attached both of the log files [tunnel.log](tunnel.log) & [conn.log](conn.log) , which I have used .)***

* Collect tunnel log files from Zeek IDS (e.g., .log, .txt)
* Ensure logs include:
* Timestamp
* Source IP
* Destination IP
* Tunnel protocol (GRE, IPv4, IPv6)
* Store files in a Splunk-accessible directory

  >***2. Add Data in Splunk***

* Log in to Splunk Web
* Navigate to Settings → Add Data
  
<img width="182" height="183" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-30 at 11 26 09 AM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/638dc249-1cc7-4bd5-81fd-fd353505c6fa" />

* Select Upload, & Upload both of the files [tunnel.log](tunnel.log) & [conn.log](conn.log) seperately for better analysis.

  >***3. Select File***

**Click Select File and upload the tunnel log file**

  >***4. Configure Source Type & don't forget to Set Sourcetype = _json & rename accordingly***

<img width="353" height="227" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 10 31 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09c66a02-2d26-49d8-bafc-4ca0bd42f1e5" />

**In my case for [tunnel.log](tunnel.log) ,I named the sourcetype as tunnel.log**

**&**

**for [conn.log](conn.log), I named it to conn.log**

**Use an appropriate sourcetype (e.g., bro, zeek_tunnel, or custom)**

  >***5. Review Settings***

* Verify index, host, and sourcetype
* Ensure proper configuration

**For [tunnel.log](tunnel.log) :**

<img width="283" height="193" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 12 27 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5ada5d1c-2ecb-47bf-88be-391cdb8dd98d" />

**For [conn.log](conn.log) : **

<img width="282" height="193" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 13 12 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/296b83b2-9395-47d4-9d39-a705e20b890f" />

   >***6. Upload Data***

**Click Review → Submit to ingest logs**

  >***7. Verify Upload***

      index=<your_tunnel_index> sourcetype=<your_tunnel_sourcetype>
      
**For [tunnel.log](tunnel.log) :**

<img width="1343" height="533" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 23 33 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/095e87e4-5f3e-41f7-b5c4-01109f4fba77" />

**For [conn.log](conn.log) :**

<img width="1360" height="536" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 23 47 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f71659c-ad1a-4871-a89b-178239c662c1" />

# Analyzing Tunnel Log Traffic

* 1. Search for Tunnel Events

         index=* sourcetype=tunnel_sample

* 2. Extract Relevant Fields

**Focus on:**

> 1. src_ip
> 2. dest_ip
> 3. tunnel_protocol
> 4. timestamp

***Example:***

> * For [tunnel.log](tunnel.log)

<img width="1345" height="395" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 27 00 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6db5537-9a71-400a-9eb8-7f2f322bb374" />

     index=main sourcetype=tunnel.log
     | rex field=_raw "\"id.orig_h\":\s*\"(?<src_ip>[^\"]+)\""
     | rex field=_raw "\"id.resp_h\":\s*\"(?<dest_ip>[^\"]+)\""
     | rex field=_raw "\"tunnel_type\":\s*\"(?<tunnel_protocol>[^\"]+)\""
     | rex field=_raw "\"ts\":\s*(?<timestamp>[0-9\.]+)"
     | table timestamp, src_ip, dest_ip, tunnel_protocol
     
> * For [conn.log](conn.log)

<img width="1348" height="407" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 29 45 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe341e84-9a20-4e55-b17d-cea03c475001" />

     index=main sourcetype=conn.log
     | rex field=_raw "\"id.orig_h\":\s*\"(?<src_ip>[^\"]+)\""
     | rex field=_raw "\"id.resp_h\":\s*\"(?<dest_ip>[^\"]+)\""
     | rex field=_raw "\"service\":\s*\"(?<tunnel_protocol>[^\"]+)\""
     | rex field=_raw "\"ts\":\s*(?<timestamp>[0-9\.]+)"
     | table timestamp, src_ip, dest_ip, tunnel_protocol


* 3. Analyze Tunneling Protocol Usage For Both Sources
     
<img width="1360" height="290" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 31 39 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1b52a4e0-3b7c-4646-a78e-7263a9d188b2" />

         index=main (sourcetype=conn.log OR sourcetype=tunnel.log)
         | eval tunnel_protocol=coalesce(tunnel_type, service)
         | stats count by tunnel_protocol

**Helps identify most used protocols**

* 4. Monitor GRE Traffic


         index=main (sourcetype=conn.log OR sourcetype=tunnel.log)
         | eval src_ip=id.orig_h, dest_ip=id.resp_h
         | eval tunnel_protocol=coalesce(tunnel_type, service)
         | search tunnel_protocol="*teredo*" OR tunnel_protocol="Tunnel::TEREDO"
         | stats count by src_ip, dest_ip

     *(No GRE traffic was observed in the dataset. Instead, Teredo tunneling (IPv6 over IPv4) was detected and analyzed.)*

**Detect unusual communication patterns**

* 5. Detect Anomalies
 
 **For Both Log files**

  <img width="1357" height="281" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 36 49 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82422229-0b20-46a3-a6f1-6079f08fa657" />

         index=main (sourcetype=conn.log OR sourcetype=tunnel.log)
         | eval tunnel_protocol=coalesce(tunnel_type, service)
         | search tunnel_protocol="*teredo*"
         | timechart span=1h count

**Sudden spikes may indicate suspicious tunneling**

* 6. Track Tunneling Activity Over Time
 
  **For Both Log files**

<img width="1357" height="253" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-31 at 11 38 05 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cc8e35ad-8949-4128-84f6-0de2813cac18" />

         index=main (sourcetype=conn.log OR sourcetype=tunnel.log)
         | eval tunnel_protocol=coalesce(tunnel_type, service)
         | timechart span=1h count by tunnel_protocol

**Compare protocol trends**
